zh.lifeSummary: 當Barbara Bacon於大約1553年出生在英國倫敦時，她的父親Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry38歲，母親Lady Anne Drury33歲。她於1577年8月31日與Sir James Calthorpe在英國素福爾可賀瑟特結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1639年11月5日在英國諾福爾可考可索普享年87歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可考可索普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Barbara Bacon于大约1553年出生在英国倫敦时，她的父亲Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry38岁，母亲Lady Anne Drury33岁。她于1577年8月31日与Sir James Calthorpe在英国素福爾可賀瑟特结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1639年11月5日在英国諾福爾可考可索普享年87岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可考可索普。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Barbara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

variants.name: Barbra
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Barbara Bacon was born about 1553, in London, England, her father, Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry, was 38 and her mother, Lady Anne Drury, was 33. She married Sir James Calthorpe on 31 August 1577, in Hessett, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 5 November 1639, in Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England, at the age of 87, and was buried in Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Barbara Bacon est née environ 1553, à Londres, Angleterre, son père, Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne Drury, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Sir James Calthorpe le 31 août 1577, à Hessett, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 novembre 1639, à Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterrée à Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Barbara Bacon um 1553 in London, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry 38 und ihre Mutter Lady Anne Drury 33. Sie hat am 31. August 1577 in Hessett, Suffolk, England Sir James Calthorpe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 5. November 1639 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England und wurde in Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Barbara Bacon è nata il circa 1553 nel London, England, sua padre, Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Lady Anne Drury, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Sir James Calthorpe il 31 agosto 1577, nel Hessett, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 5 novembre 1639, nel Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England, all'età di 87 anni, e fu sepolta a Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Barbara Baconが約1553年にイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewryは38歳，母Lady Anne Druryは33歳であった。1577年8月31日にイギリススフォルクヘセットでSir James Calthorpeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1639年11月5日にイギリスノーフォルクコクソルペで87歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクコクソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Barbara Bacon(이)가 대략 1553년 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Anne Drury(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1577년 8월 31일 영국 수폴크 헤싯에서 Sir James Calthorpe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1639년 11월 5일 영국 노르폴크 콕소에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 콕소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Barbara Bacon nasceu aproximadamente 1553, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Lady Anne Drury, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir James Calthorpe em 31 de agosto de 1577, em Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 5 de novembro de 1639, em Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 87 anos, e foi sepultada em Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Barbara Bacon родилась около 1553 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne Drury, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James Calthorpe 31 августа 1577 г. в местности хессетт, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 5 ноября 1639 г. в местности кокктаурп, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 87 лет и была похоронена в местности кокктаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Barbara Bacon nació aproximadamente 1553, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Francis Bacon of Hessett Drewry, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Lady Anne Drury, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Sir James Calthorpe el 31 de agosto de 1577, en Hessett, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 5 de noviembre de 1639, en Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultada en Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
